19.reason: [Operator precedence error: '=' has lower precedence than '!=', so it evaluates as c = (fgetc(in) != EOF), which assigns 0 or 1 to c instead of the character]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Operator precedence error: '=' has lower precedence than '!=', so it evaluates as c = (fgetc(in) != EOF), which assigns 0 or 1 to c instead of the character]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Operator precedence error: assignment happens after comparison, so c gets 0 or 1 instead of the actual character]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Operator precedence error: assignment 'c = fgetc(in)' should be parenthesized because '!=' has higher precedence than '=', causing c to be assigned the result of comparison (0 or 1), not the character]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Operator precedence error: '=' has lower precedence than '!=', so it evaluates as c = (fgetc(in) != EOF), making c either 0 or 1, not the actual character]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Operator precedence error: '=' has lower precedence than '!=', so it evaluates as c = (fgetc(in) != EOF), which assigns 0 or 1 to c instead of the character]

63.reason: [Incorrect comparison logic: should return 0 when strings are equal; this breaks strict weak ordering required by qsort]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect comparison logic: should return 0 when strings are equal; this breaks strict weak ordering required by qsort]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect comparison logic: should return 0 when equal, and positive only when greater; breaks qsort contract]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect comparison logic: when strings are equal, strcmp returns 0, but this function returns 1, violating strict weak ordering required by qsort; should return strcmp(da->s, db->s) directly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect comparison logic: should return 0 when strings are equal; current implementation breaks strict weak ordering required by qsort]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect comparison logic: should return 0 when strings are equal; this breaks qsort's requirement for a proper comparator]

31.reason: [Incorrect usage: strcpy expects a string literal or valid source; better to use s[0] = '\0';]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect usage: strcpy expects a string literal or valid source; better to use s[0] = '\0';]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect usage: strcpy(s, "\000") only copies one null byte; should use memset(s, 0, sizeof(s)) or s[0] = '\0'; but more critically, this line is redundant since next read will overwrite, and the real issue is the loop condition]
